definition_origin: COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Act, 1998, FTC enforcement; Rule 16 CFR Part 312) regulates online collection of personal information from children under 13 in the US. The amended COPPA Rule was finalized by the FTC on April 22, 2025 and published in the Federal Register; compliance deadline is April 22, 2026. The 2025 amendments expanded the definition of 'personal information' to include persistent identifiers (IDFA, GAID, cookies), biometric data (face / fingerprint / voice), precise geolocation, and behavioral / inferred data (interest-based profiles, browsing history). The amendments also tightened verifiable parental consent (VPC) requirements, mandated explicit parental consent for behavioral advertising to under-13 users, and required separate parental consent for disclosure to third-party operators. Enforcement is real: Disney $10M FTC settlement (December 2025) for COPPA violations across multiple Disney apps; Epic Games $245M settlement (Dec 2022) for Fortnite; TikTok $5.7M (2019) — and FTC has signaled aggressive enforcement under the new rule. Mixed-audience apps (apps not directed to children but accessed by children) must use neutral age gates per FTC guidance.
core_mechanism: Three regulatory mechanisms. (1) NEUTRAL AGE GATE — apps must use age-neutral collection (date-of-birth picker), NOT 'Are you over 13?' yes/no (which encourages lying). FTC guidance: birthdate gate AND silent under-13 routing. (2) UNDER-13 BRANCH — if calculated age < 13: STOP standard onboarding, route to verifiable parental consent (VPC) flow; do NOT collect any personal info from the child until parental consent verified. (3) VPC METHODS (16 CFR § 312.5(b)) — FTC-approved methods: government-issued ID verification, credit card verification + small charge, phone call to trained personnel, video conference, knowledge-based authentication, signed consent form. The 2025 amendments expanded approved methods to include facial-recognition-against-government-ID. After VPC, the app may collect minimum-necessary data with parent oversight (parent dashboard for review/delete). Under-16 EU users have parallel GDPR-K (Article 8) requirements (varies 13-16 by member state). UK Children's Code (in force 2 September 2021) overlays additional protections (high-privacy default, no nudges, age-appropriate language).
empirical_evidence: FTC enforcement under COPPA: Disney $10M settlement December 2025 (multiple Disney apps including Disney+ Kids profile, ESPN Fantasy, miscellaneous); Epic Games $245M settlement Dec 2022 (Fortnite — children's data + dark-pattern privacy practices); TikTok $5.7M 2019 (Musical.ly acquisition); YouTube $170M 2019 (children's data); Amazon $25M 2023 (Alexa retained children voice data); Microsoft $20M 2023 (Xbox children accounts). FTC 2025 amended rule rationale: existing rule did not capture modern data types (persistent IDs, behavioral inference, biometric); enforcement gap demonstrated by Disney + Epic settlements. Pew Research 2024: 67% of teens 13-17 admit lying about age when signing up for apps that asked age — supports FTC's neutral-gate guidance over yes/no. UK ICO Children's Code enforcement 2024-2025: investigations of TikTok, Snapchat, Instagram, Roblox; Standard 13 (Nudge Techniques) cited. EU GDPR-K Art 8: parental consent for under-16 (member states can lower to 13). Italian Garante TikTok €10M fine 2021 for inadequate age verification. UNESCO 2024 Children Privacy report: 162 countries have age-verification provisions, but only 41 have COPPA-equivalent technical compliance standards.
measurable_outcome_metric: Age gate completion rate, under-13 detection rate (% of users routed to VPC), VPC completion rate (% who finish parental consent), abandonment rate at VPC (drop-off measurement), parent dashboard usage rate, COPPA-compliance audit score (qualitative).
conversion_lift_benchmark: Not a conversion-lift item — defensive compliance. Negative outcome: Disney $10M (Dec 2025), Epic $245M (Dec 2022), TikTok $5.7M, YouTube $170M, Amazon $25M, Microsoft $20M. State penalties + class actions stack on top. Reputational damage substantial (Epic settlement was front-page WSJ for weeks).
engineering_tactics: ARCHITECTURAL CHANGES: (1) Replace yes/no age gate with NEUTRAL DATE-OF-BIRTH picker as first onboarding screen (before any data collection). FTC guidance: 'Are you 13 or older?' encourages lying; date-of-birth picker is neutral. (2) Calculate age client-side: if (age < 13) { routeToCOPPAFlow() } — DO NOT collect any other data first. (3) COPPA flow: explain parental consent required, capture parent email, send VPC verification link via Postmark/SendGrid. (4) VPC method: implement at least one FTC-approved method — typical for indie: credit-card + $0.50 charge (refunded immediately) OR government-ID upload + Persona/Onfido/Stripe Identity verification ($1-3 per check). (5) Persistent identifiers (IDFA, GAID, ad-cookies) PROHIBITED for under-13 users without VPC — must disable third-party SDKs (Meta, Google Ads) for child profiles. (6) Behavioral advertising prohibited for under-13 (2025 amendments). (7) Parent dashboard: separate flow for parent to review collected data, delete, revoke consent (Art 13 + 17 GDPR equivalent). (8) Mixed-audience flag: in App Store Connect, declare app as 'Not Made for Kids' if mixed-audience OR 'Made for Kids' if directed (must use Apple Kids Category compliance, restricts monetization). Implementation pattern (RN): const dob = await showDatePicker(); const age = differenceInYears(new Date(), dob); if (age < 13) { await routeToCOPPAFlow(parentEmail) } else if (age < 16 && isEU) { await routeToGDPRKFlow(parentEmail) } else { proceedStandardOnboarding() }. Server-side: log age_gate_decision + lawful_basis for audit trail.
real_world_examples: 1) Apple's age verification for Apple ID (Family Sharing) — gold-standard government-grade VPC. 2) Roblox — neutral date-of-birth gate; under-13 routes to Roblox Studio kids version with VPC required for chat / DM features. 3) YouTube Kids — separate app with full COPPA compliance; no behavioral advertising. 4) Disney+ — Kids Profile mode; no behavioral ads for kids profiles per Dec 2025 settlement. 5) Snapchat — neutral DOB; under-13 blocked; under-18 limited features. 6) TikTok — post-Italian Garante 2021 redesign: stricter age verification; under-18 default-private. 7) Cal AI — declares 'Not Made for Kids' (App Store Connect); date-of-birth gate required for medical-disclaimer compliance separately. 8) Counter-example: Disney pre-2025 settlement — multiple apps (Disney+, ESPN Fantasy) failed to disable persistent identifiers for kids profiles; $10M FTC fine. 9) Counter-example: Fortnite Epic Games — $245M for default-public chat + behavioral profile of under-13 users. 10) UK Children's Code compliant: BBC iPlayer Kids, CBBC apps — age-appropriate UI + privacy default-high.
mobile_vs_web_diffs: Mobile: Apple App Store 'Made for Kids' category (strict — no third-party analytics, no behavioral ads, app review +1-2 weeks); 'Not Made for Kids' default. Google Play 'Designed for Families' equivalent. iOS Family Sharing + Screen Time provide platform-level kids protections. Web: parental verification harder (no Apple Family Sharing infrastructure); Persona / Onfido / Stripe Identity are common VPC tools. UK ICO Children's Code applies to web equally.
platform_design_language_alignment: Apple HIG Kids Category: bold typography, simplified UI, age-appropriate language. Material 3 for Android: equivalent kids-mode patterns. UK Children's Code Standard 13 prohibits 'Nudge techniques' — limits dark-pattern engagement design. Liquid Glass / M3 Expressive can be used for kids UIs but typography simplified, motion reduced (children cognitive load).
category_fit: MANDATORY for: kids/family apps (directed to children), educational kids apps, gaming with under-13 users, social with under-13 users. CRITICAL: mixed-audience apps (general consumer apps that children also use — wellness, social, gaming) must implement neutral age gate to avoid COPPA exposure. EXCLUDE: B2B apps (corporate users); over-18 apps (dating, gambling — different regulation); apps that legitimately exclude under-13 via App Store age rating + actual gate.
solo_dev_feasibility: 1_week for compliant age gate + COPPA branch (without full VPC). ~1 month for full VPC + parent dashboard + government-ID verification integration. Solo-dev shortcut: declare 'Not Made for Kids' in App Store Connect + neutral date-of-birth gate + block under-13 entirely (refuse account creation, show 'must be 13+' message). This is FTC-compliant if app is genuinely not directed to children — under-13 detection + refusal is the simplest path. Full VPC required only if app intends to serve under-13 users. Dependencies: Persona / Onfido / Stripe Identity ($1-3 per check) for full VPC, Postmark/SendGrid for parent verification email, separate parent-dashboard infrastructure.
tooling_options: VPC providers: Persona, Onfido, Stripe Identity, Veriff, Jumio (government-ID + facial-recognition matching, ~$1-3 per check). Apple Family Sharing API (iOS, free, native). Google Play Kids approval process (free, native review). UK ICO Children's Code self-assessment toolkit (free). PRIVO, kidSAFE certified compliance services. Hopscotch / SuperAwesome for kids-specific advertising / monetization (alternative to behavioral ads). Postmark / SendGrid for parent verification emails. Persona / OneTrust for parent dashboard.
ai_dependency: none for compliance mechanism; optional for AI-driven age estimation (controversial — Apple has explicit guidance against using AI age inference as primary age gate; FTC ambivalent).
claude_design_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Implement COPPA-compliant age gate + VPC branching in React Native. STEP 1: First onboarding screen = NEUTRAL date-of-birth picker (NOT yes/no Are you 13+? — FTC says encourages lying). STEP 2: Calculate age client-side; route: under 13 = COPPA flow (parental consent before any data collection); EU under-16 = GDPR-K flow; EU 16+ / US 13+ = standard onboarding. STEP 3: COPPA flow: explain parental consent required, capture parent email, Postmark sends VPC verification link, Persona/Stripe Identity verifies parent ID + facial recognition match. STEP 4: After VPC, minimum-necessary data only; persistent identifiers (IDFA, GAID, ad-cookies) DISABLED for under-13 users; behavioral advertising PROHIBITED (2025 amendments). STEP 5: Parent dashboard accessible from Settings — review / delete / revoke consent (mirror GDPR Art 17). Forbid: yes/no age gate, asking under-13 personal info before VPC, behavioral ads to under-13, third-party tracking SDKs for under-13 profiles. App Store Connect: declare Not Made for Kids OR follow Made for Kids strict requirements.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Implement the under-13 refuse path (simpler than full VPC for non-kids apps). After date-of-birth gate, if age < 13: show We are sorry, but you must be 13 or older to use this app. Apple-style empty state with simple Cabinet Grotesk + Manrope typography. Forbid: collecting any data, allowing app access, retargeting via persistent ID. Server-side log: age_gate_decision = under_13_refused (audit trail).'
stitch_application: Prompt fragment 1: 'Generate the neutral COPPA age gate as the FIRST onboarding screen. Layout: header Cabinet Grotesk 28pt bold left-aligned: When were you born? Subhead Manrope 16pt: This helps us show you appropriate content. Date-of-birth picker iOS native style (month / day / year wheels). Primary CTA Continue 56pt height. Privacy notice link Manrope 14pt grey-700: We follow COPPA + GDPR. Read our Privacy Notice. Forbid: yes/no Are you 13+? format, asking name/email before age, indigo-500, fake urgency.' Prompt fragment 2: 'Generate the VPC parental consent flow. Step 1 screen: header Söhne 24pt bold: We need your parents permission. Subhead Manrope 16pt: Please ask a parent or guardian to enter their email. Primary parent email input field 56pt height. CTA Send verification link 56pt. Step 2 screen (parent email side): receives Postmark email with link to web VPC flow (Persona ID verification + facial recognition match against government ID). Step 3 (after VPC): parent dashboard with toggles for what app can collect / show / share — all default OFF (UK Children Code high-privacy default).'
ai_codegen_failure_mode: AI defaults: (1) Yes/no age gate (Are you 13+?) — encourages lying, FTC explicitly disfavors. (2) Asks name/email/profile BEFORE age gate (collects under-13 data without VPC = direct COPPA violation). (3) Under-13 detected but proceeds with standard onboarding. (4) Persistent identifiers (IDFA, GAID) NOT disabled for under-13 profiles (2025 amendments + Disney $10M precedent). (5) Behavioral advertising fires for under-13 (2025 amendments prohibit). (6) Third-party SDKs (Meta, Google Ads, AppsFlyer) initialized for under-13 (data sharing without parent consent). (7) Parent dashboard missing or buried (Art 17 + COPPA equivalent rights violated). (8) App Store Connect declared Made for Kids without disabling all third-party SDKs (Apple Kids Category strict prohibits). Counter-prompt: 'NEUTRAL date-of-birth picker as FIRST onboarding screen (not yes/no). Under-13 routes to VPC flow OR refuse-with-message; do NOT collect data first. For mixed-audience apps, the safest path is: declare Not Made for Kids in App Store Connect + neutral DOB gate + refuse under-13 with kind message. For apps serving under-13: full VPC via Persona/Stripe Identity, parent dashboard, persistent IDs DISABLED, behavioral ads PROHIBITED, third-party SDKs DISABLED. App Store Connect declarations must match implementation.'
anti_patterns: 1) Yes/no age gate (FTC disfavors). 2) Collecting data before age gate. 3) Persistent IDs NOT disabled for under-13 (2025 amendments + Disney precedent). 4) Behavioral ads to under-13 (prohibited). 5) Third-party SDKs (Meta, Google Ads) initialized for under-13. 6) Parent dashboard buried or missing. 7) App Store 'Made for Kids' declaration with non-compliant SDK use. 8) Default-public profile for under-13. 9) Engagement-maximizing design for under-18 (UK Children's Code Standard 13). 10) Friend-discovery / DM features default-on for under-13. 11) Failure to inform parent of data collected post-VPC.
regulatory_risk: HIGH. FTC COPPA enforcement: Disney $10M (Dec 2025), Epic $245M (Dec 2022), TikTok $5.7M, YouTube $170M, Amazon $25M, Microsoft $20M — multi-million-to-$245M range. State AG enforcement (CA, NY, MA particularly active). EU GDPR-K Art 8: equivalent enforcement (Italian Garante TikTok €10M 2021, Replika €5M Feb 2025). UK ICO Children's Code: investigations of TikTok, Snapchat, Roblox 2024-2025. Brazil LGPD-K. India DPDPA child protections. Apple App Store Guideline 1.3 — Kids Category strict requirements; 5.1.4 — kids privacy. Google Play Designed for Families requirements.
platform_constraints_2026: FTC amended COPPA Rule effective 2026-04-22 (compliance deadline). Apple App Store Guideline 1.3 + 5.1.4 — Kids Category prohibits third-party advertising, third-party analytics, in-app purchases without VPC. Google Play Designed for Families program requirements. EU GDPR-K Art 8 (parental consent under 16, member states can lower to 13). UK Children's Code (Sept 2021+). Apple iOS 17+ Communication Limits + Family Sharing.
accessibility_implications: Date-of-birth picker must be keyboard accessible (WCAG 2.2 SC 2.1.1) + VoiceOver/TalkBack-friendly (each picker wheel labeled). Refuse-message screen for under-13 must be empathic, not punitive (UX care principle). Parent dashboard must meet WCAG 2.2 AA. Children's UI (if Kids Category) must use larger touch targets (≥48x48pt), simpler language (8th grade reading level max).
pitfalls_caveats: 1) Date-of-birth lying — neutral gate doesn't prevent it; FTC accepts neutral-gate implementation as 'reasonable measure'. 2) Mixed-audience determination is fact-specific (FTC factor analysis: subject matter, visual content, language, music, characters, ads). 3) VPC method choice trade-offs: government-ID stricter but higher friction; credit-card lower friction but excludes parents without cards. 4) State-level minor laws stacking (CA Age-Appropriate Design Code Sept 2024 — ruled unconstitutional in part by 9th Cir. but partial provisions remain; UT, CT, TX). 5) International: GDPR-K varies 13-16 by member state. 6) Parent dashboard data-export to parent may conflict with child's evolving privacy interests (UN Convention on Rights of the Child). 7) Behavioral inference from non-PII may still be 'personal information' under 2025 amendments. 8) Apple App Store Kids Category review +1-2 weeks longer than standard.
cross_cultural_validity: EU GDPR-K Art 8: 16 default; member states can lower (DE 16, FR 15, IT 14, ES 14, IE 16, NL 16, PL 13). UK Children's Code 2021. US COPPA federal + state laws (CA, UT, CT, TX). Brazil LGPD-K. Korea PIPA + Youth Protection Act. India DPDPA child protections. Japan APPI. Australia Privacy Act review 2024-2025 with proposed children-specific provisions. Apply COPPA + UK Children's Code + EU GDPR-K as strictest baseline globally. RTL: layout mirror.
prescribed_action: Implement age gate as FIRST onboarding screen: (1) Neutral date-of-birth picker (NOT yes/no). (2) Client-side age calculation; route: under-13 US = COPPA flow (full VPC OR refuse-with-message); under-16 EU = GDPR-K flow; 13+ US / 16+ EU = standard. (3) For mixed-audience apps not serving under-13: declare 'Not Made for Kids' in App Store Connect + refuse-with-message under-13. (4) For apps serving under-13: full VPC via Persona/Stripe Identity, parent dashboard accessible from Settings (≤2 taps), persistent IDs DISABLED for under-13, behavioral ads PROHIBITED, third-party SDKs (Meta/Google Ads/AppsFlyer) DISABLED for under-13, all default privacy = high (UK Children's Code Standard 7). (5) Server-side audit log: age_gate_decision + lawful_basis + VPC_method + timestamp. (6) Comply with 2026-04-22 deadline for amended COPPA Rule.
master_prompt_relevance: 06_monetization_compliance (primary), 04_implementation (age gate + VPC + parent dashboard wiring), 02_design (age-appropriate UI for kids paths), 03_architecture (age-conditional state-machine routing).
questionnaire_relevance: Age gate is the FIRST screen — questionnaire MUST come AFTER. For under-13 users post-VPC: questionnaire must be minimum-necessary, age-appropriate copy, no behavioral profiling questions, parental dashboard surface for parent oversight.
aha_relevance: N/A directly. Indirect: compliant age gate prevents the entire app from being a regulatory liability and protects user trust.
telemetry_events: age_gate_shown, age_gate_completed (age_band: under_13 / under_16_eu / standard), coppa_flow_entered, vpc_email_sent, vpc_completed (method: id_verification / credit_card / etc), under_13_refused, parent_dashboard_viewed, persistent_id_disabled_for_minor, behavioral_ad_suppressed_for_minor.
measurement_horizon: Day 0 (compliance gate at age verification). Ongoing (parent dashboard usage, VPC renewal). Annual (compliance audit; FTC enforcement awareness). Compliance deadline 2026-04-22 — must be in place before then.
emotional_archetype_alignment: Caregiver (protect children = ultimate caregiver act), Sage (informed age-appropriate framing), Innocent (honest neutral gate). Conflicts with Magician / Outlaw / Hero archetypes if those interpret child protection as friction — but compliance is non-negotiable; archetype voice can warm the disclosure.
anti_pattern_paired: Pairs against: Yes/No Age Gate (encourages lying), Data Collection Before Age Gate, Persistent IDs for Under-13 (Disney precedent), Behavioral Ads to Under-13 (2025 amendments), Hidden Parent Dashboard, Mixed-Audience Without Neutral Gate. Aligns with: GDPR Data Minimization (item 46), CMP / ATT Framework (item 47), DFA Audit (item 49, kids protection overlap).
sources: [Loeb Loeb: Children's Online Privacy in 2025 — Amended COPPA Rule](https://www.loeb.com/en/insights/publications/2025/05/childrens-online-privacy-in-2025-the-amended-coppa-rule)
